Japanese: １０月１８日　午前１０時 地方裁判所　第４法廷
English: October 18, 10:00 AM District Court Courtroom No. 4

Japanese: これより、荷星　三郎の 法廷を開廷します。
English: The court is now in session for the trial of Mr. Will Powers.

Japanese: 検察側、 準備完了しております。
English: The prosecution is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: 弁護側、 準備完了しております。
English: The defense is ready, Your Honor.

Japanese: 御剣検事。 冒頭弁論をおねがいします。
English: Very well. Mr. Edgeworth, your opening statement, please.

Japanese: 被告人・荷星　三郎は、 １０月１５日、午後２時３０分、
English: The prosecution will show the court that at 2:30 PM on October 15, the defendant,

Japanese: 英都撮影所・第１スタジオで、 俳優・衣袋　武志を殺害した。
English: Mr. Will Powers, killed fellow actor Jack Hammer at Studio One of Global Studios.

Japanese: この犯行は、被告人以外の 人間には不可能だった。
English: It is impossible for anyone else to have committed this heinous crime.

Japanese: 本法廷で、検察側はその点を 完全に立証するだろう。
English: The evidence presented during the trial will all point to this fact.

Japanese: ふむぅ。‥‥なるほど。
English: Hmm. I see.

Japanese: それでは、 審議に入りたいと思います。
English: Very well, I would like to move on to your testimony.

Japanese: 御剣検事、最初の証人を 入廷させてください。
English: Mr. Edgeworth, the prosecution may call its first witness.

Japanese: では、まず、おなじみ イトノコギリ刑事を。
English: First, I would like to call a familiar face, Detective Gumshoe, to the stand.

Japanese: まず、この事件について、 かるく説明してほしい。
English: Detective, if you would briefly describe this case to the court?

Japanese: はッ。 では、上面図で説明するッス。
English: Yes sir! I'll explain with the guidemap here.

Japanese: 今回は、撮影所全体の位置関係が 重要ッス。
English: To understand this case, it's important to grasp the layout of the studios, see?

Japanese: ここがスタッフエリア。
English: This here is the Employee Area.

Japanese: 午前中、ここでアクションの 打ち合わせがあったッス。
English: The actors did a run-through of their action scenes during the morning here.

Japanese: ここが、撮影所の正門前。
English: This is the main gate to the studios.

Japanese: 午後１時から、警備のオバチャンが ずっと立っていたッス。
English: The security lady that works at the studios was here at 1:00 PM on that day.

Japanese: 警備員の詰所を過ぎると、 ゲートがあるッス。
English: Past the security station there is a gate, see?

Japanese: ここを過ぎると、 スタジオがあるッス。
English: Past that are the studios.

Japanese: ここッス。 ‥‥第１スタジオ‥‥。
English: And here it is... Studio One.

Japanese: 死体が発見された、殺人現場ッス。
English: This is the scene of the murder, where the body was found!

Japanese: さて、事件があった１０月１５日。 午前中、この撮影所には、
English: Now, on the day of the murder, October 15, there were only three people here.

Japanese: 被害者の衣袋　武志、
English: The victim, Jack Hammer,

Japanese: 被告人の荷星　三郎、
English: the defendant, Will Powers,

Japanese: スタッフの女の子の、 ３人しかいなかったッス。
English: and a young woman, the production assistant.

Japanese: 正午まで、撮影所のスタッフは 全員、スタッフエリアにいたッス。
English: All the production staff were in the Employee Area until noon.

Japanese: そして昼食後、被害者の 衣袋は、１人で第１スタジオへ。
English: Then after lunch, the victim, Jack Hammer, went to Studio One.

Japanese: 午後１時、警備のオバチャンが 詰所に入ったッス。
English: Right after that, at 1:00 PM, the security lady got to the guard station.

Japanese: そして、午後５時。
English: Now, jump ahead to later that day... 5:00 PM.

Japanese: 衣装をつけたリハーサルを するため、
English: The production staff came to Studio One to perform a rehearsal.

Japanese: 第１スタジオにスタッフたちが 集まると‥‥
English: Needless to say, the rehearsal was cancelled...

Japanese: ‥‥被害者の死亡推定時刻は、 午後２時３０分ッス。 2:30 PM.
English: The time of death was

Japanese: 凶器は、胸に突き刺さった 《トノサマン・スピアー》ッス。
English: The "Samurai Spear" found lodged in the victim's chest was the murder weapon.

Japanese: これが、事件の流れッス。
English: That's the case, in brief.

Japanese: ‥‥もう１回、聞きたい人が いたら、説明するッスけど‥‥？
English: Anyone like to hear that again?

Japanese: （どうしよう。 もう一度、聞こうか‥‥？）
English: (Hmm... should I listen to that whole thing again...?)

Japanese: すみません。 ‥‥もう１回、おねがいします。
English: I'm sorry, could you describe that one more time?

Japanese: まぁたアンタッスか。
English: I shoulda known it would be you, pal!

Japanese: しかたないッス。アタマのワルい 弁護士のために、くりかえすッス。
English: Fine. Once more for the mentally-challenged lawyer.

Japanese: （ここぞとばかりにイヤミなんか 言ってるよ‥‥）
English: (Yeah, yeah. Live it up while you can, Gumshoe.)

Japanese: （まあ、なんとかなるだろう‥‥）
English: (I think I can probably remember that...)

Japanese: 凶器は、《ヤリ》ですか。 ‥‥これはまた、ブッソウな‥‥。
English: So the murder weapon was a "spear"?
How... medieval!

Japanese: 証拠品《トノサマン・スピアー》を 法廷記録にファイルした。
English: Samurai Spear added to the Court Record.

Japanese: よろしいか。裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: この事件は、実にシンプルな ポイントにまとめることができる。
English: This case is quite simple if you ask one question:

Japanese: すなわち。
English: And that question is:

Japanese: 《警備詰所にいたオバチャンは、 ナニを見たか‥‥？》
English: "What did the security lady at the guard station see?"

Japanese: ‥‥なるほど。
English: Understood.

Japanese: ではさっそく、その警備員に 入廷していただこう。
English: Let's call this security officer to the stand!

Japanese: 証人の名前を。
English: Will the witness declare her name?

Japanese: あら。
English: Hmm?

Japanese: ちょいとアンタ、イイ男だね！
English: My, aren't you a handsome fellow!

Japanese: オバチャン、ちょっと トキめいちゃったョ。
English: I'm afraid I'm a bit flustered!

Japanese: な、名前を聞いている！
English: Y-your name, please!

Japanese: ヤダねこの子、テレちゃってサ。 ‥‥“オバチャン”でいいわョ。
English: Oh, dearie! No need for you to be embarrassed! Just call me "grandma."

Japanese: たのむから、名前をッ！
English: YOUR NAME, PLEASE!

Japanese: （御剣のやつ、証人から 名前を聞くのがニガテらしいな）
English: (Seems Edgeworth has a bit of trouble getting his witnesses to say their names...)

Japanese: 大場　カオル（おおばかおる）ョ。
English: Wendy Oldbag, dearie.

Japanese: “オオバサン”も“オバチャン”も たいして変わらないでショ。
English: So just call me "grandma." It's practically my name!

Japanese: そうそう、オバチャンもホラ、若い ころからオバチャンだったワケじゃ
English: So even when I was young I was an Oldbag, but not really that was just my name dearie.

Japanese: ないでしょだからねえムカシはよく イジメられたりもしたワケよこれで
English: Still how the other children would make fun of me and just because of my name can you

Japanese: 中学生のころアコガレていた将棋部 のセンパイがいてねェそのカレにサ
English: believe it? But there was this boy, the captain of the chess club in junior high, and when

Japanese: オバチャン呼ばわりされたときは もうオバチャン泣いちゃったわねェ
English: he called me an old bag well I just cried and cried because I had a crush on him you see--

Japanese: しょ‥‥証人のオシャベリに 異議を申し立てる！
English: O-objection! I... object to the witness's talkativeness.

Japanese: 異議を認めます。
English: Objection sustained!

Japanese: 証人は、オシャベリを やめるように。
English: The witness will refrain from rambling on the stand.

Japanese: これからオモシロくなる ところだったのに‥‥。
English: I was just getting to the good part, dearie!

Japanese: では、 証言を始めていただく。
English: Perhaps we can get to the testimony?

Japanese: 証人は、事件当日。正門前に 詰めていたのですね？
English: Now, the witness was stationed at the main gate on the day of the murder, correct?

Japanese: そうョ。
English: Yessey I was.

Japanese: 殺人現場へ行くには、あなたの 目の前を通るしかない？
English: And to get to the scene of the murder, someone would have to pass by you?

Japanese: 話がわかるオッチャンだねェ！
English: You know your stuff, dearie!

Japanese: 証言をおねがいしましょう。
English: You may begin your testimony.

Japanese: （“オバチャンパワー” おそるべし‥‥！）
English: (She sure is one hell of an Oldbag...)

Japanese: ～事件当日について～
English: -- Witness's Account --

Japanese: オバチャン、事件があった日は、 午後１時に詰所に入ったのョ。
English: On the day of the murder, I arrived at the guard station at 1:00 PM.

Japanese: イブクロちゃんたちは、午前中から 打ち合わせしてたんだけど。
English: Poor old Hammer and the rest had been doing a run-through there since the morning.

Japanese: オバチャン、ちょっと午前中は ‥‥その、用事があってサ。
English: I, well, I had some errands to run that morning.

Japanese: ま、とにかく、午後１時に 詰所に入ったわけョ。
English: Anyway, it was 1:00 when I got to the guard station.

Japanese: それから、午後５時までは ず～っと正門のあたりにいたわネ。
English: I was at the main gate from then until 5:00!

Japanese: イブクロちゃんの事件があったの、 午後２時３０分ごろでショ？
English: Now, the murder happened at 2:30 PM, right?

Japanese: ２時ごろにね。オバチャンの 目の前を通った男がいたのよ。
English: Interesting to me, because a certain man walked right by me at 2:00 PM.

Japanese: 荷星よ荷星。あの男がね。 スタジオの方へ歩いていったのサ。
English: It was Powers! That man right there, and he was heading toward the studio!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 被告人がねぇ‥‥。
English: You saw the defendant, then? Hmm...

Japanese: では。さっそく《尋問》を してもらいましょうか。
English: Very well. Let's begin the cross-examination. Mr. Wright?

Japanese: はい。
English: Yes, Your Honor.

Japanese: ～事件当日について～
English: -- Witness's Account --

Japanese: オバチャン、事件があった日は、 午後１時に詰所に入ったのョ。
English: On the day of the murder, I arrived at the guard station at 1:00 PM.

Japanese: イブクロちゃんたちは、午前中から 打ち合わせしてたんだけど。
English: Poor old Hammer and the rest had been doing a run-through there since the morning.

Japanese: オバチャン、ちょっと午前中は ‥‥その、用事があってサ。
English: I, well, I had some errands to run that morning.

Japanese: ま、とにかく、午後１時に 詰所に入ったわけョ。
English: Anyway, it was 1:00 when I got to the guard station.

Japanese: それから、午後５時までは ず～っと正門のあたりにいたわネ。
English: I was at the main gate from then until 5:00!

Japanese: イブクロちゃんの事件があったの、 午後２時３０分ごろでショ？
English: Now, the murder happened at 2:30 PM, right?

Japanese: ２時ごろにね。オバチャンの 目の前を通った男がいたのよ。
English: Interesting to me, because a certain man walked right by me at 2:00 PM.

Japanese: 荷星よ荷星。あの男がね。 スタジオの方へ歩いていったのサ。
English: It was Powers! That man right there, and he was heading toward the studio!

Japanese: なるほどくん。
English: Nick...

Japanese: なんか、あのオバチャン、 ミョーにイキイキしてるね‥‥。
English: Doesn't she seem like she's enjoying this a little too much?

Japanese: オバチャンといえば、人の不幸と ウワサ話にゃ目がないからね。
English: Windbags love gossiping about other people's misfortunes.

Japanese: ‥‥やだぁ、そんなのぉ‥‥。
English: Ugh. Can't she get her kicks somewhere else?

Japanese: その時間まで、スタジオには 誰でも入れたんですか？
English: So up until that time, anyone who wanted to could go into the studio?

Japanese: たしかに警備員は いなかったけどね。
English: Well, there wasn't a security guard at the gate, no.

Japanese: 正門にロックを かけておいたから、
English: But the main gate was locked, dearie.

Japanese: スタッフ用のカードキーが ないと、入れないんだョ。
English: You'd need an employee cardkey to get in.

Japanese: （ああ。あのカードか‥‥。 ぼくも持ってるな‥‥）
English: (Right... the cardkey that I have.)

Japanese: （つまり、撮影所に関係ない人は 入れなかったワケだ‥‥）
English: (So, what she's saying is no one who wasn't supposed to be there could get in.)

Japanese: 証人が詰所に入ったとき、 スタジオには誰がいたのか？
English: Was there anyone at the studios when you arrived at the guard station?

Japanese: “打ち合わせ”‥‥？
English: A "run-through"...?

Japanese: トノサマンとアクダイカーンの 対決シーンのね。
English: They were working out a scene between the Steel Samurai and the Evil Magistrate.

Japanese: 午前中に大まかな動きを 決めて、午後は練習時間さ。
English: They got the basics figured out by noon, and were to use the afternoon for practice.

Japanese: ５時からリハーサルっていう 段取りだったからね。
English: Rehearsal was supposed to start at 5:00.

Japanese: だから、イブクロちゃんは午後、 第１スタジオに行ったワケ。
English: That's why poor old Hammer went to Studio One in the afternoon.

Japanese: （練習時間なのに、荷星さんは 楽屋でヒルネ‥‥か）
English: (So Powers was in his dressing room sleeping... when he was supposed to be practicing!)

Japanese: （ノンキなヒーローだな‥‥）
English: (Talk about a laid-back hero...)

Japanese: 打ち合わせは午前中だったのに、 なぜ証人は午後から？
English: If there was a run-through in the morning, why did you only get there after noon?

Japanese: なんの用事ですか？
English: What kind of "errands"?

Japanese: いや‥‥ちょっとサ。
English: Oh, well, you know.

Japanese: 答えてください！
English: Answer the question!

Japanese: 実はネ。見物してたのョ。 イブクロちゃんと荷星をサ。
English: Actually... I was watching poor Hammer and Powers. Observing, you know?

Japanese: け、見物‥‥？ 警備をほったらかして？
English: O-observing? Weren't you supposed to be at the guard station?

Japanese: うるさい子だね！　だから、 ちゃんと正門はカギかけといたョ！
English: Whippersnapper! I told you I locked the gate, didn't I? I did my job!

Japanese: （そういう問題じゃ ないような気がするけど‥‥）
English: (I'm not sure your boss would see it that way...)

Japanese: とにかく！
English: Anyway!

Japanese: イブクロちゃんの打ち合わせは、 ゼッタイ見ることにしてるのョ。
English: I always watch Hammer's run-throughs. Always!

Japanese: これだけは、ゆずれないネ！
English: Never missed one in all my years.

Japanese: いいなぁ。アタシも見たいなあ‥‥ トノサマンの打ち合わせ。
English: Wow! I want to see a Steel Samurai run-through too!

Japanese: 霊媒師やめて、警備員に なったら‥‥？
English: You could quit being a spirit medium and take up guard duty.

Japanese: そうしようかなぁ‥‥。
English: Not a bad idea...

Japanese: 被害者のすがたは見ましたか？
English: Did you see the victim then?

Japanese: イブクロちゃん？ ああ、見てないよ。
English: Did I see poor old Hammer? Nope.

Japanese: オバチャンが詰所にもどる前に、 もうスタジオへ行ったみたいだネ。
English: He'd already gone to the studio before I got back to the guard station.

Japanese: それから、証人はどうしたか？
English: Tell us what happened next.

Japanese: １秒も目を離さず 見ていたんですか？
English: So you were watching there the WHOLE time? No breaks? Not even a second?

Japanese: あ‥‥アッタリマエでしょ！ オバチャンはプロなのョ！
English: O-of course! I am a professional, you know!

Japanese: それともナニ！　オバチャンの シゴトにケチをつける気！
English: Or are you criticizing how I do my job!? Whippersnapper!

Japanese: だって昨日は、詰所の中で ようかんを‥‥
English: No, it's just, after seeing you eating those donuts in the guard station yesterday...

Japanese: ようかん食べてても、 ちゃんと見てたわョ！
English: I can watch the gate and eat donuts at the same time!

Japanese: おととい来やがれ！
English: Snipperwhapper!

Japanese: “おととい”“来やがれ”‥‥？
English: "Snipperwhapper"...?

Japanese: ‥‥言ってることが ムジュンしてるよ、なるほどくん。
English: Old windbag is losing it, Nick.

Japanese: 異議があったら、あのオバチャンに 言ってやってよ。
English: Why don't you raise an objection?

Japanese: とにかく証人は、キチンと 見張りをしていた、と。
English: So, the witness was on duty through the afternoon, the whole time. Very well.

Japanese: ‥‥で。何を見たんですか？
English: What did you see?

Japanese: なぜ、それを知ってるんですか？
English: How did you know that?

Japanese: いやネ。コートを着た 暑苦しい刑事がいるだろ？
English: Oh hoh hoh. I have my ways! That over-dressed young detective told me.

Japanese: ようかん食わせてやったら、 なんでもしゃべってくれたよ。
English: I fed him some donuts and he talked and talked...

Japanese: プロのオバチャンに言わせたら。 ダメだね。あの刑事は。
English: If you ask me, that detective isn't cut out for his job.

Japanese: （カワイソウな イトノコ刑事‥‥）
English: (Poor Detective Gumshoe, getting chewed out by a security guard...)

Japanese: ２時‥‥ですか！
English: 2:00? You're sure?

Japanese: そうだよ。
English: Sure as spit!

Japanese: その男とは、ダレだったんですか！
English: Well, who was the man!?

Japanese: いい年コイて、みっともなく あわてるんじゃないョアンタ。
English: Really, you should try not to get so excited at your age. You might strain something.

Japanese: まあ、そうヘコまないで。 ‥‥教えてやるからサ！
English: Now, now, don't get your skivvies all in a bunch. I'll tell you who it was!

Japanese: 荷星さんを見たんですか？
English: So you saw Mr. Powers?

Japanese: クドいネ！ そう言ってるだろ！
English: That's what I said! Got wax in your ears, sonny?

Japanese: なるほどくん。 今がチャンス、だね。
English: Nick, this is your chance!

Japanese: ああ。このバクダンを ぶつけてやるか‥‥。
English: Yeah, time to pull out the secret weapon...

Japanese: オバチャン、何回でも 言っちゃうよ！
English: I'll say it again! It was him!
Let me get this straight, old bag! Er, Ms. Oldbag!

Japanese: あなたは昨日から、何度も “荷星さんを見た”と言ってます！
English: You've been saying since yesterday that you "saw Mr. Powers," correct?

Japanese: その“荷星さん”とは、 この写真の男のことですか！
English: But you're talking about the man in this photo, aren't you!

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと待ちなさい。 ‥‥成歩堂くん。
English: J-just a moment, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: その写真、私にも見せてください！
English: Let me see that photo!

Japanese: なんですか‥‥これ。
English: What is this, exactly?

Japanese: ネオ・エドシティを守る男、 トノサマンその人です。
English: None other than the Steel Samurai, defender of Neo Olde Tokyo.

Japanese: オバチャン！
English: Ms. Oldbag!

Japanese: あなたが見た“荷星さん”とは、 この人物のことですか！
English: Is this the "Mr. Powers" that you saw!?

Japanese: 決まってるでショ！
English: Of course! Didn't your momma teach you any sense, sonny?

Japanese: ダレが見たって荷星じゃない。 ねえ、アンタ。
English: Anyone can plainly see that's Powers! Right?

Japanese: ‥‥えッ！
English: Um, yeah.

Japanese: あー、うむ。 ‥‥ど、どんなものだろうか。
English: Umm... well. I wonder?

Japanese: たしかに、荷星さんは トノサマンの役をやっています！
English: True, Mr. Powers does play the role of the Steel Samurai!

Japanese: しかし、荷星さんは トノサマンじゃない！
English: But that doesn't mean Mr. Powers IS the Steel Samurai!

Japanese: わ‥‥わかってるわョ！
English: I... I know that! I wasn't born yesterday!

Japanese: ええと‥‥そのぉ、証人。
English: No one in this court is accusing you of that, Ms... er, witness.

Japanese: （“オバチャン”と呼ぶには 抵抗があるらしいな‥‥）
English: (He's having trouble calling her "Oldbag" apparently...)

Japanese: この人物が、被告人・荷星さん だという証拠は、ないんですか？
English: However, you do not have proof that the person in this photo is Mr. Will Powers, do you?

Japanese: やだネ、 うたぐり深いオッチャンは。
English: Humph! Nosy old man!

Japanese: あるに決まってるじゃない、証拠。
English: Of course I have proof!

Japanese: なにっ！
English: What!?

Japanese: えええっ！
English: Huh!?

Japanese: （なんで御剣まで おどろいてるんだよ‥‥）
English: (Even Edgeworth is surprised!?)

Japanese: オバ‥‥‥証人！
English: The prosecution would like to ask the old... the witness.

Japanese: 知っていることは、ちゃんと 前もって話しておいてもらいたい！
English: Please make known all the information in your possession ahead of time!

Japanese: ナニ言ってんのよ！
English: How was I to know everyone would be so nosy! You should be ashamed, all of you!

Japanese: オバチャン、この写真を刑事に 見せてあげたのに、
English: Anyway, I showed that photo to the young detective.

Japanese: “こんなの、証拠にならんから いらねッス”って、
English: He told me "this isn't any good as evidence, pal."

Japanese: 見向きもしなかったんじゃない！
English: He didn't even give it a second look!

Japanese: うむむ。
（御剣も、オバチャンには カタナシだな‥‥）
English: (Wow, old windbag has left even Edgeworth speechless. She's good!)

Japanese: では。《証言》をして いただきましょう。
English: Let's hear about your proof, then.

Japanese: ～写真の男は荷星である～
English: --The Man in the Photo--

Japanese: オバチャンね、イイカゲンなこと 言うの、大ッキライなのョ。
English: I never say anything I don't mean, mind you!

Japanese: 午前中、スタッフエリアで 打ち合わせしたんだけどサ。
English: That morning, during the run-through of the action scene...

Japanese: その打ち合わせのとき、荷星のヤツ ころんじゃったんだよ。
English: I saw Powers trip and fall!

Japanese: 大事な小道具もこわれちゃうし、 ちょっと大変だったね。
English: He broke one of the props, it was a big mess.

Japanese: 荷星も、思いっきり足を ヒネっちゃってさァ。
English: Apparently, he sprained his ankle pretty bad.

Japanese: この写真、よく見なさいョ！
English: Now, look at that picture!

Japanese: ちゃんと写ってるだろ？ ‥‥足を引きずってるところがさ。
English: You can see he's dragging his leg! See? Clear as day!

Japanese: だから、コイツは荷星なんだョ。 わかったかい？
English: That's how I knew it was Powers. Happy?

Japanese: ふむう、足にケガを していたわけですか‥‥。
English: Hmm. So he had sprained his ankle...?

Japanese: では、成歩堂くん。 ‥‥《尋問》を‥‥。
English: Very well. Mr. Wright, you may cross-examine the witness.

Japanese: （とにかく、ゆさぶりをかけて 情報を探り出そう‥‥）
English: (She's got to be hiding something... I'll press her until she squeals.)

Japanese: ～写真の男は荷星である～
English: --The Man in the Photo--

Japanese: オバチャンね、イイカゲンなこと 言うの、大ッキライなのョ。
English: I never say anything I don't mean, mind you!

Japanese: 午前中、スタッフエリアで 打ち合わせしたんだけどサ。
English: That morning, during the run-through of the action scene...

Japanese: その打ち合わせのとき、荷星のヤツ ころんじゃったんだよ。
English: I saw Powers trip and fall!

Japanese: 大事な小道具もこわれちゃうし、 ちょっと大変だったね。
English: He broke one of the props, it was a big mess.

Japanese: 荷星も、思いっきり足を ヒネっちゃってさァ。
English: Apparently, he sprained his ankle pretty bad.

Japanese: 写真にも、ちゃんと写ってるだろ？ ‥‥足を引きずってるところがさ。
English: You can see he's dragging his leg! See? Clear as day!

Japanese: だから、コイツは荷星なんだョ。 わかったかい？
English: That's how I knew it was Powers. Happy?

Japanese: どう？　なるほどくん。 ‥‥ムジュンしてるところ、ある？
English: Well, Nick? Find any glaring contradictions?

Japanese: とりあえず、この写真の男が 荷星さんだと思い込んでいるから、
English: Well, she seems pretty sure that was Powers in that photo...

Japanese: どんどんゆさぶって、 情報を集めてみようか‥‥。
English: I guess I'll just press her and see if I can shake her conviction...

Japanese: えーと‥‥
English: Umm...

Japanese: おだまりッ！
English: Whippersnapper!

Japanese: （まだ何も言ってないぞ‥‥）
English: (Hey, I haven't said anything yet!)

Japanese: 言っとくけどオバチャンはね、 人サマに後ろユビさされるような、
English: I'll have you know I'm not pointing fingers at anyone behind their backs!

Japanese: そんなイイカゲンなことは、 何ひとつ、してないんだからネ！
English: Everything I've said is on the straight and narrow! The up and up!

Japanese: だいたい今の若いヒトはアレだね いつでも他人のアラ探しばっかり
English: Youth today! Always whining about each other, pointing fingers this way and that!

Japanese: オバチャンはそれがカナシイわョ そもそも‥‥
English: It's enough to make an old lady want to cry...

Japanese: 弁護人！
English: Mr. Wright!

Japanese: たのむから、この証人を シゲキしないでほしい！
English: Please, for all our sakes, try not to upset the witness!

Japanese: （やれやれ‥‥）
English: (I think Edgeworth has met his match...)

Japanese: 打ち合わせをしたメンバーは、 誰と誰ですか‥‥？
English: Who was present at the run-through?

Japanese: え。え、えーと‥‥ トノサマンの荷星だろ。
English: Well, let's see... There was Powers, he's the Steel Samurai...

Japanese: アクダイカーンのイブクロちゃん。
English: Then poor old Hammer, the Evil Magistrate.

Japanese: ‥‥そして、オバチャン。
English: And... me.

Japanese: 証人は、何を‥‥？
English: And what exactly were you doing?

Japanese: 見物。‥‥見てただけ。
English: Observing! Just... observing.

Japanese: スタッフの女の子は？
English: What about the assistant?

Japanese: ああ。あの子は大道具の 片づけをしてたわョ。
English: Oh, she was off moving backdrops around and such.

Japanese: （あの子、打ち合わせは 見ていなかったのか‥‥）
English: (So she didn't see the run-through, then...)

Japanese: “ころんだ”‥‥？
English: "Trip and fall"...?

Japanese: そうだよ。トノサマンのくせに、 マヌケったらありゃしないョ。
English: Yes! And to think, he's supposed to be the Steel Samurai! What a laugh!

Japanese: 荷星のやつ、 足をひねっちゃってさ。
English: So Powers sprained his ankle.

Japanese: しかたない、オバチャンが 手当てしてあげたわョ。
English: I helped make it better for him, of course.

Japanese: 手当て‥‥ですか？
English: You... helped make it better?

Japanese: ツバつけてやった。
English: I kissed it where it hurt.

Japanese: そ、それはそれは。
English: L-let's just skip over that part, shall we?

Japanese: そのとき、スタッフの女の子は？
English: Where was the assistant then?

Japanese: ああ。あの子はずっと大道具を 片づけていたからね。
English: Oh, her? She was cleaning up backdrops, I think.

Japanese: 荷星のケガのことは知らないョ。
English: She didn't know about Powers's ankle.

Japanese: （ふうん‥‥）
では、証言をつづけていただこう。
English: You may continue your testimony.

Japanese: 小道具がこわれたんですか？
English: He broke a prop?

Japanese: ああ。 トノサマン・スピアーがね。
English: Sure did! His own Samurai Spear.

Japanese: （トノサマン・スピアー？ 凶器じゃないか！）
English: (His Samurai Spear... the murder weapon!?)

Japanese: オバチャンがね。とりあえず ガムテープで修理したんだけどさ。
English: Luckily I was there with my duct tape to fix it.

Japanese: （ふうん‥‥。それって、 けっこう重要なんじゃあ？）
English: (This strikes me as a significant detail...)

Japanese: （法廷記録にメモしておくか‥‥）
English: (I'd better write this down in the Court Record.)

Japanese: 証拠品《トノサマン・スピアー》の データを書き直した。
English: Samurai Spear updated in the Court Record.

Japanese: 荷星さん、 ヒドいケガだったんですか？
English: Was Mr. Power's ankle badly sprained?

Japanese: 動けないほどじゃァ なかったんだけどさ。
English: Not so bad that he couldn't walk around.

Japanese: とりあえず、ヒルメシ食べたあと、 少し楽屋で休むことになったね。
English: He went to his dressing room to rest up after lunch.

Japanese: （それで荷星さん、午後は、 ヒルネしてたわけか‥‥）
English: (Thus the nap...)

Japanese: とにかく、歩くときは 足を引きずっていたよ。
English: Anyway, I saw him dragging his foot when he walked.

Japanese: （足を引きずっていた‥‥）
English: (Dragging his foot... okay.)

Japanese: ほんとですか？ （ちょっと写真を見てみよう‥‥）
English: Are you absolutely sure? (Let's take a look at that picture again...)

Japanese: （‥‥歩き方が不自然なのは、 ケガをしていたからなのか‥‥）
English: (Hmm... so he was walking strangely because he was injured...)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。たしかに、 足を引きずっていますね‥‥。
English: Hmm. It does look like he was dragging his foot.

Japanese: ほぉら！ ‥‥オバチャン、ウソつかない！
English: See! And you didn't trust me! Whippersnapper!

Japanese: でも、見た目はトノサマン じゃないですか！
English: Still, you can't look at this and say it's Mr. Powers!

Japanese: しかし、足を引きずっていたのは、 被告人・荷星しかいない！
English: Yes, but only Mr. Powers would have been dragging his leg!

Japanese: そうですね‥‥。荷星さんと 考えるのが自然でしょう‥‥。
English: He has a point. It does seem quite likely that it was Powers.

Japanese: （とりあえず、もっとゆさぶりを かけて、情報を集めよう‥‥）
English: (There's got to be more to this. I'd better keep pressing her.)

Japanese: そこまでッ！
English: I think we've heard enough!

Japanese: ‥‥いかがだろうか。 裁判長。
English: Haven't we, Your Honor?

Japanese: １つ、気になることが あるのですが‥‥？
English: Well, there is one thing that bothers me.

Japanese: なんだろうか？
English: Which is?

Japanese: その“トノサマン”の着ぐるみは、 今、どこにあるんですか？
English: Where is this "Steel Samurai" costume now?

Japanese: う‥‥うむ。
English: Umm... hmm.

Japanese: それが、実は。 どこにも‥‥見あたらなくて、
English: Actually, well... We couldn't find it.

Japanese: 現在、捜索中である。
English: We're looking, though.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: とにかく！
English: Anyway, that's not important!

Japanese: 証人が見たのは、たしかに “トノサマン”ではあるが‥‥
English: The witness did see the Steel Samurai, yes.

Japanese: その中に入っていたのは、 被告人・荷星としか考えられない。
English: And it is clear that the person in the Steel Samurai suit was Mr. Will Powers!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥そうですね‥‥。
English: Hmm... I suppose that's right.

Japanese: （裁判長が迷っているぞ‥‥）
English: (Are you sure you're sure, Your Honor?)

Japanese: （とりあえず、 ようすを見よう‥‥）
English: (I think I'll wait and see where this goes.)

Japanese: たしかに、この日、撮影所には スタッフがいなかったため、
English: I suppose, since there weren't any additional crew members at the studios that day...

Japanese: この着ぐるみの中に、被告人が 入っていた可能性が高い。
English: It's quite likely that it was the defendant inside that costume.

Japanese: ‥‥そう考えて問題ないでしょう。
English: I see no problem with this evidence.

Japanese: ご理解、カンシャする‥‥。
English: Most perceptive, Your Honor!

Japanese: （むむ‥‥裁判長の心証が 悪くなっちまったぞ‥‥）
English: (He still doesn't seem that convinced to me... I hope.)

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！
English: Hold it right there!

Japanese: 可能性が高かろうが、トノサマンは トノサマンです。
English: We keep talking possibilities, but we have to agree that this photo shows the Steel Samurai!

Japanese: 証人は、荷星さんのすがたは 見ていない！
English: Nowhere in this photo can we see Mr. Will Powers!

Japanese: ‥‥ふむう！
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 弁護人の言うとおりですね。
English: The defense has a point.

Japanese: それに、この写真の人物の他に、 犯行のチャンスはなかったのか？
English: I also wonder if someone else not caught on camera could have killed Mr. Hammer.

Japanese: ‥‥という問題もあります。
English: We have to consider that possibility, also.

Japanese: では次に、その点をあきらかにして お目にかけよう‥‥。
English: Then allow me to remove that doubt from your mind, Your Honor...

Japanese: 証人。‥‥続けて証言を していただこう。
English: Will the witness continue her testimony, please?

Japanese: まかしときなョ！
English: No need to ask twice!

Japanese: ～事件当日について・つづき～
English: -- Witness's Account, cont.--

Japanese: イブクロちゃんが死んだ時間、 午後２時３０分だったよネ。
English: The time of poor Hammer's death was 2:30 PM, true?

Japanese: その時間までに、オバチャンが見た 人間は、この荷星だけだョ。
English: The only person I saw go to the studio before then was Will Powers!

Japanese: 他には誰も、スタジオへ行った ヤツぁいないんだ。
English: No one else went there!

Japanese: もしいたら、このオバチャンが 見ているはずだからネ！
English: If they had, I would have seen them!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: もし、スタジオに向かったのが この人物だけならば、
English: So, if no one else went to the studio...

Japanese: このトノサマンこそが、 犯人ということになりますが‥‥。
English: Then it would have to be this "Steel Samurai" who did it.

Japanese: では、成歩堂くん。 ‥‥《尋問》を。
English: Mr. Wright, you may cross- examine the witness.

Japanese: ～事件当日について・つづき～
English: -- Witness's Account, cont.--

Japanese: イブクロちゃんが死んだ時間は、 午後２時３０分だったよネ。
English: The time of poor Hammer's death was 2:30 PM, true?

Japanese: その時間までに、オバチャンが見た 人間は、この荷星だけだョ。
English: The only person I saw go to the studio before then was Will Powers!

Japanese: 他には誰も、スタジオへ行った ヤツぁいないんだ。
English: No one else went there!

Japanese: もしいたら、このオバチャンが 見ているはずだからネ！
English: If they had, I would have seen them!

Japanese: あのオバチャンも、 よく言うよねー‥‥。
English: Old windbag sure has balls! Or... well, you know what I mean.

Japanese: まあ、カン違いと思い込みは、 オバチャンのトレードマークだし。
English: Old windbags have a way of skipping over important details, after all...

Japanese: ついでに、もの忘れも。
English: Or, for that matter, entirely forgetting them.

Japanese: やだぁ、そんなの‥‥。
English: How convenient.

Japanese: （自分の未来のすがたを かさねているみたいだな‥‥）
English: (Something for you to look forward to, perhaps?)

Japanese: なんでそれを知ってるんですか？
English: How do you know that?

Japanese: あれ？　さっき言わなかったっけ？
English: Eh? Didn't I just explain?

Japanese: コートを着た刑事にさ、 ようかん食わせてやったのョ。
English: I gave donuts to the young detective.

Japanese: そしたら、“秘密ッスよ”とか 言いつつ全部しゃべってくれたワ。
English: And then, after some rubbish about it being a "secret, pal" he told me everything.

Japanese: （“イトノコ刑事は ようかんがお好き”‥‥か）
English: (I see Detective Gumshoe shares his fellow officer's fondness for donuts.)

Japanese: 間違いありませんか！
English: Are you absolutely sure!?

Japanese: ああん、ないネ。あやしいヤツぁ 通らなかったョ。
English: Oh, quite, quite, sonny. Nobody suspicious lookin' passed by at all.

Japanese: （あやしいヤツ‥‥ねえ‥‥）
English: (Nobody suspicious lookin'... Right.)

Japanese: オバチャンが詰所に入る 前は、どうですか！
English: What about before you got to the guard station?

Japanese: 午後１時より前のことかい？
English: You mean before 1:00?

Japanese: そのときは、みんな スタッフエリアにいたからネ。
English: Why, everyone was in the Employee Area before then.

Japanese: 正門はカギかけちゃってたから、 誰も入って来られないし。
English: And I locked the main gate so no one could get in.

Japanese: それにさァ。
English: Not to mention...

Japanese: ゼッタイですか！
English: Are you sure!?

Japanese: 証人はすでに、答えている！
English: The witness has answered the question!

Japanese: スタジオに行くには、証人の 目の前を通るしかないのだ！
English: Anyone going into the studio had to pass by the witness!

Japanese: そうだョ！
English: That's right, sonny!

Japanese: もっと言ってやってよ！ ミッちゃん！
English: You let 'em have it, Edgey-boy!

Japanese: （ミ‥‥ミッちゃん。 ミツルギ、だからか‥‥）
English: (E-Edgey-boy...?)

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！
English: Hold on!

Japanese: もう一度、この写真について カクニンさせてください。
English: Look at this photograph one more time.

Japanese: これは、スタジオに通じるゲートの カメラが撮った写真ですね？
English: This photo was taken by the camera at the gate for the studios, correct?

Japanese: ああ、そうだョ。
English: Yesiree, sonny.

Japanese: 誰かがここを通ると、カメラが 自動的に写真を撮るんですね？
English: So, whenever anyone passes by here, it automatically takes a picture?

Japanese: よく知ってるネ？
English: And here I thought you didn't know your head from a hole in the ground.

Japanese: ‥‥そして。
English: Right... Anyway!

Japanese: 写真のデータは、詰所の パソコンに記録されます。
English: It's also true that the computer in the guard station records all security cam data.

Japanese: アンタもウチで はたらくかい？
English: Ooh! You know, if you wanted to work at the studio, we might have an opening.

Japanese: この写真は、そのパソコンで プリントアウトしたものです。
English: That very computer printed out this photo.

Japanese: ウラに《１０ガツ１５ニチ ゴゴ２ジ　２ニンメ》とあります。
English: Note that on the back of the photo are printed the words: [Oct 15, 2:00 PM, Photo #2]

Japanese: そりゃアンタ“午後２時に撮った 写真”ってことだョ。
English: Even I know that means it was taken at 2:00 PM on October the 15th! Really, sonny!

Japanese: そんなことはわかっています。
English: Actually, I knew that too.

Japanese: 問題は、“２ニンメ”の部分です。
English: The issue here is the bit at the end where it says: "Photo #2"...

Japanese: パソコンには、写真のデータは これ１枚きりでした。
English: The computer only held data for one photo on that day.

Japanese: それならば‥‥
English: Don't you think that's odd?

Japanese: この写真には《１ニンメ》と 書かれていなければおかしい！
English: Shouldn't this photo be "Photo #1" if it really was the ONLY photo!?

Japanese: 静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: 成歩堂くん！ どういうことですか！
English: Please tell the court what you mean by this, Mr. Wright!

Japanese: ぼくの方が聞きたいですね。
English: Actually, that's what I want to ask the witness.

Japanese: この証拠品は、“２人の人間”が スタジオに行ったことを証明する。
English: This evidence shows that not one, but two people went to the studio that day.

Japanese: しかし、写真のデータは１枚しか 残っていない。
English: Yet there is only data for one of the photos!

Japanese: そのデータを消したのは‥‥
English: Who could have erased the data for the other photo?

Japanese: オバチャンしかいない！
English: Only someone with access... the security lady herself!
Eh!?!

Japanese: オバチャン、知らないよッ！
English: You watch your mouth, whippersnapper!

Japanese: オバチャン、あの日は荷星しか 見てないんだからね！
English: The only person I saw that day was Will Powers!

Japanese: しかし、ゲートのカメラは “２人通った”ことを示している！
English: But the camera on the gate fired twice! That means two people went by!

Japanese: ううん‥‥まあ、 そういうことになるねェ‥‥。
English: Umm... well, yes... that's what it would seem to mean...

Japanese: どういうことですか？‥‥証人。
English: Can the witness explain this to the court?

Japanese: ‥‥‥‥うう。 オバチャンに聞かれても‥‥。
English: Umm... ahem. I-I don't understand these new-fangled computer things...

Japanese: ちょ、ちょっと、ミッちゃん！ ‥‥助けてちょうだいョ！
English: Umm... E-Edgey-boy! Help!

Japanese: えッ！
English: Huh!?

Japanese: カ、カンベンしていただく。 ‥‥私も知りたい。
English: B-believe me, I want to, but I don't know what this means either.

Japanese: フン！ かわいげのない子だよ。
English: Humph! Some help you are! You're a whippersnapper too!
Whippersnapper...?

Japanese: どうしましたか？ 証人。
English: Something the matter, Ms. Oldbag?

Japanese: そ、そうだった！
English: Ah! That's right!

Japanese: ‥‥オバチャン、 思い出しちゃったョ！ I...
English: I just remembered something!

Japanese: もしかして‥‥。
English: Let me guess...

Japanese: このトノサマンの他に、 誰か通った人がいたのですか？
English: Someone else passed by the gate... someone other than the Steel Samurai?

Japanese: うう‥‥ん。 ま、そういうことになるのかねェ。
English: Er... well, yes, I suppose you could put it that way.

Japanese: ふう‥‥。やれやれ。
English: *sigh* I see.

Japanese: では、《証言》を。
English: Your testimony, please.

Japanese: ～“もう１人の人物”のこと～
English: --The Other Person--

Japanese: オバチャン、その日の警備が 終わると、もうひとシゴトあるの。
English: Every day, after I finish my guard duties, I have one other important job to do.

Japanese: パソコンに記録された写真を チェックするんだけどサ。
English: I go through the photos recorded on the security computer and check them.

Japanese: あやしくない写真のデータは、 オバチャン、消しちゃうのョ。
English: I throw out any photos that aren't suspicious lookin', you see.

Japanese: そういえば、事件の日、 １枚、消した覚えがあるョ！
English: Come to think of it, now I remember throwing out one photo that day!

Japanese: しょ、証人！ そんなコト、初めて聞いたぞ！
English: M-Ms. Oldbag! This is the first I've heard of this!

Japanese: そりゃそうでしょ。 今、思い出したんだからサ。
English: Well, of course, sonny! I've only just remembered it.

Japanese: と、とにかく、成歩堂くん。 《尋問》を‥‥。
English: Right... anyway, Mr. Wright, please begin the cross-examination.

Japanese: なんかまた、スゴいことに なってきたね‥‥。
English: Well, I'd say this was a turn for the unexpected, but I kind of expected this...

Japanese: ～“もう１人の人物”のこと～
English: --The Other Person--

Japanese: オバチャン、その日の警備が 終わると、もうひとシゴトあるの。
English: Every day, after I finish my guard duties, I have one other important job to do.

Japanese: パソコンに記録された写真を チェックするんだけどサ。
English: I go through the photos recorded on the security computer and check them.

Japanese: あやしくない写真のデータは、 オバチャン、消しちゃうのョ。
English: I throw out any photos that aren't suspicious lookin', you see.

Japanese: そういえば、事件の日、 １枚、消した覚えがあるョ！
English: Come to think of it, now I remember throwing out one photo that day!

Japanese: こ、こんな大事なコト、 “そういえば”で済んじゃうの？
English: "Come to think of it"? Couldn't she think of that a little earlier!?

Japanese: 何度も言うけど、それが オバチャン、てもんだろ？
English: Classic old windbag maneuver, Maya.

Japanese: ふーん‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: それはちょっと、ベンリかも。
English: Might be a useful tactic to remember for use in my future career as a windbag.

Japanese: （とにかく、“消した”という 写真について聞いてみるか）
English: (Let's find out more about this "erased" photo.)

Japanese: “ひとシゴト？”
English: Another "job"?

Japanese: そ。その日、正門前を通った 人間を、全部チェックすんの。
English: T-that's right. I check all the people that passed by the main gate that day.

Japanese: ゲートのカメラが撮った写真の データも、キチンと見るわよ。
English: I look closely at every one of the photos that security camera takes.

Japanese: 写真のデータは、すべて 保存しておくのだろうか？
English: Do you backup all of the security camera data?

Japanese: いちおう、そうするように 言われてるんだけどさァ。
English: Well, they keep telling me too, yes.

Japanese: それじゃあ、ヤヤこしいでしょお？
English: But those computers are just so frustrating.

Japanese: 写真って、何枚ぐらい あるものなんですか？
English: How many photos are there, usually?

Japanese: 一般公開日なんかは ５００人を超すこともあるけど。
English: On a day when we're open to the public, we get more than 500 people.

Japanese: 撮影やリハーサルに関しちゃ、 お金がないカラね、ウチは。
English: But on filming days or rehearsal days... well, we don't have much money.

Japanese: ５０人超すことはめったにないョ。
English: We never get more than 50, and that's a big day.

Japanese: あ、“あやしくない”って、 どうやってわかるんですか？
English: How exactly do you determine what isn't "suspicious lookin'"?

Japanese: そんなの、カオ見りゃ わかるでしょ、だいたい。
English: Oh, you can tell by looking at their faces. For the most part.

Japanese: だ、だいたい‥‥って。
English: F-for the most part?

Japanese: 殺人事件なんですよ！
English: This is a murder trial, Ms. Oldbag!

Japanese: だから、あやしい荷星の写真は 残しといたし、いいでショ！
English: Well, I didn't touch that suspicious lookin' Will Powers's photo, did I?

Japanese: （‥‥いいのか？）
English: (I think she's missing the point.)

Japanese: そ、その“消した”１枚には、 誰が写っていたんですか！
English: Well, who in the heck was in that photo you erased!?

Japanese: ファンだョ。
English: Humph. A fanboy.

Japanese: ファン？
English: F-fanboy?

Japanese: トノサマンのサ。 熱狂的な‥‥ネ。
English: Steel Samurai fanboys. Real freaks, if you ask me.

Japanese: リハーサルの情報をどこからか 仕入れてきて、
English: They get information about the rehearsals from gosh- knows-where.

Japanese: ウロチョロする連中がいてねェ。 ‥‥あの日も、１人‥‥。
English: They're always hanging about. One was there that day.

Japanese: ちょっと待ってください！ さっき証人はこう言ったはずです！
English: W-wait a second! Didn't you just say no one else could get in!?

Japanese: “撮影所の正門にはカギをかけた から、誰も入ってこなかった”
English: "I locked the main gate so no one could get in"... Those were your words!

Japanese: いやネ。
English: Well!

Japanese: スタッフエリアにサ、 排水口があるんだけど。
English: If you must know, there's a drain that goes into the Employee Area.

Japanese: あそこ、金網が はずれちゃっててねェ。
English: The grate has been loose for a while.

Japanese: アレ、そとにつながっててサ。 あそこから入ってくるんだョ。
English: It leads outside, and well, that's where they come in.

Japanese: あ、あんなところから 入ってくるんですか？
English: They come in through the drain?

Japanese: 言ったろ？　相手は “熱狂的なファン”なの。
English: I told you they were freaks.

Japanese: ‥‥それに、
English: Oh, and...

Japanese: それに？
English: And...?

Japanese: ‥‥コドモだからね。
English: They're kids. Children. Whippersnappers.

Japanese: こ、子供ッ！
English: K-kids!?

Japanese: じゃ、じゃあ！　オバチャンが 消した写真に写ってたのは‥‥？
English: So, on the photo that you erased...?

Japanese: 男の子だョ。 ‥‥小学校２、３年生ぐらいの。
English: It was a boy. Probably 2nd or 3rd grade.

Japanese: ええええええええええええええッ！
English: Whaaaaaaaaaaaat!?

Japanese: せ、静粛に！　静粛に！
English: O-order! Order!

Japanese: もう一度、カクニンします。
English: Let me get this straight.

Japanese: あの日、証人の目の前を通って スタジオに向かった人物は、２人。
English: You saw two people pass by the gate on their way to the studios that day?

Japanese: １人は、足を引きずった トノサマン。
English: One was the Steel Samurai, dragging his leg.

Japanese: そしてもう１人は、 小学２、３年生の男の子。
English: The other was a boy who looked to be in about 2nd or 3rd grade?

Japanese: そうだョ。‥‥いつものことでサ。
English: Oh yes, well we see his type there every day.

Japanese: 追っかけても つかまりゃしない。
English: Can't stop 'em. Can't catch 'em.

Japanese: 小学２・３年生の少年‥‥か。
English: A boy in 2nd or 3rd grade? Hmm...

Japanese: 凶器のトノサマン・スピアーを あつかうのはムリだろうな‥‥。
English: I assume it would be hard, if not impossible for a young boy to wield the Samurai Spear?

Japanese: ムリでしょう。 あれはけっこう、重いですから。
English: Impossible, I'd think. It's quite heavy.

Japanese: ネ？　カンケイないだろ事件とは！
English: Right! As I said, I didn't pay him much mind.

Japanese: だからオバチャン、データを 消しちゃったんだョ。
English: That's why I erased the data.

Japanese: ね。なるほどくん、 どういうこと？
English: Um, Nick? What's going on?

Japanese: 犯人のコウホがもう１人、 登場したんだよね？
English: I mean, the boy was there, that makes him a suspect!

Japanese: そして今、早くも 退場していくところだ。
English: Yeah, and they're already trying to "un-suspect" him.

Japanese: ここで、５分間の 休憩をとります。
English: I'd like to take a five minute recess.

Japanese: 弁護側・検察側とも、 ただ今あきらかになった事実を、
English: I want the defense and the prosecution to consider this new information...

Japanese: 検討していただきたい！
English: And no forgetting vital information this time!

Japanese: １０月１８日　午前１１時８分 地方裁判所　被告人第３控え室
English: October 18, 11:08 AM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 3

Japanese: ‥‥ニボサブさん。
English: Mr. WP?

Japanese: は、はい。
English: Y-yes?

Japanese: 本当に、楽屋にいたんですね？ スタジオには行ってませんね？
English: Tell me straight: were you really in your dressing room? You didn't go to the studio?

Japanese: あの、行ってません！ その、寝てました！
English: I-I didn't go to the studio! I was s-sleeping, honest.

Japanese: じゃあ、写真に写っていた トノサマンは、誰なのかなあ？
English: So who was the "Steel Samurai" in that security photo?

Japanese: 知りませんよ、そんなの‥‥。
English: How should I know?

Japanese: トノサマンの着ぐるみは 楽屋のスミに置いておいたので、
English: The Steel Samurai costume was off in the corner of the dressing room.

Japanese: 持っていこうと思えば、 誰でもできたと思いますけど‥‥。
English: Anyone could have walked in and taken it, really.

Japanese: えー。 なんか、不用心ですねー。
English: What!? You should take better care of that stuff!

Japanese: まさか、着ぐるみなんか 盗む人もいないと思いまして‥‥。
English: I couldn't imagine anyone would want to steal a Steel Samurai costume!

Japanese: ‥‥あの。 ボク、どうなんでしょう？
English: So... where does this leave me?

Japanese: かなりヤバいと思いますよ。
English: It doesn't look good...

Japanese: え。
English: *sniff*

Japanese: 今のところ、犯人は あなたしか考えられませんから。
English: You're the only likely suspect, right now.

Japanese: そんなぁ‥‥！
English: Nick!

Japanese: どど、どうするの？ なるほどくん。
English: W-what are we going to do!?

Japanese: とにかく、時間をかせごう。
English: First, we play for more time.

Japanese: 荷星さんの他に、犯行が可能だった 人物を、ムリヤリ指摘するんだ！
English: We'll start targeting someone else that could conceivably have done this!

Japanese: そうか！
English: Right!

Japanese: それでゴネて、明日まで 判決をのばすんだね。
English: And it'll take them so long to shoot us down that we can get another day!

Japanese: そう。‥‥指摘を間違えると、 たぶんアウトだ。
English: Right... but if we pick the wrong person, we might lose on the spot.

Japanese: な、なんか 苦しまぎれに聞こえますね。
English: You... don't sound very optimistic.

Japanese: いや。ジッサイ、 まぎれもなく苦しいんですよ！
English: I'm not optimistic at all, actually.

Japanese: あ。なるほどくん、 ‥‥時間だよ。
English: Hey, Nick. It's time.

Japanese: じゃ、行こうか。 ふう‥‥。
English: Okay. Let's go. *sigh*

Japanese: た、ため息をつかないで ください‥‥。
English: What does that mean... Please don't sigh like that! *sniff*

Japanese: では、審理を再開します。
English: The court will now reconvene for the trial of Mr. Will Powers.

Japanese: 御剣検事。‥‥意見をどうぞ。
English: Mr. Edgeworth, will you present the prosecution's thoughts on this matter?

Japanese: 検察側の意見は、 カンタンだ。
English: The prosecution's thoughts are simple.

Japanese: 状況は、何も変わっていない。
English: Nothing has changed.

Japanese: スタジオに行った２人目の人物は、 １０才ぐらいの少年だった。
English: The other person who went to the studios was a boy of roughly 10 years of age.

Japanese: たしかに、写真は決定的な 証拠にはならないが、
English: The photo we do have may not be hard evidence...

Japanese: 他に犯行が可能だった人物は いない！
English: But there is still no one else that could have committed this crime!

Japanese: 荷星　三郎に《有罪判決》を！
English: I call for a verdict of "guilty" for the defendant, Mr. Will Powers!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: では、成歩堂弁護士。 ‥‥意見をどうぞ。
English: Very well. Mr. Wright, your thoughts?

Japanese: 弁護側の意見は、 検察側とは違います。
English: The defense disagrees with the prosecution's claim.

Japanese: 犯行が可能だった人物は、 被告人の他にもいました！
English: There IS another person who could have committed this crime!

Japanese: 静粛に。 ‥‥それはおもしろい。
English: Order! Interesting...

Japanese: さっそく聞かせていただきます。 ‥‥ただし。
English: Let us hear who you have in mind! However...

Japanese: カンケイない人物を 犯人呼ばわりした場合。
English: Be aware that this court does not look kindly on accusing the innocent.

Japanese: それなりのペナルティを与えるので そのつもりで‥‥。
English: If you accuse someone who is obviously innocent, you will be penalized.

Japanese: （しっかりクギをさされて しまった‥‥）
English: (Right. Great. As if the stakes weren't high enough.)

Japanese: 被告人・荷星さん以外に、犯行が 可能だった人物とは‥‥？
English: So, who was this person other than Mr. Powers that could have committed murder?

Japanese: それは、スタッフの女の子です！
English: It was the assistant!

Japanese: トノサマンの着ぐるみは、 荷星さんの楽屋にあった。
English: The Steel Samurai costume was in his dressing room.

Japanese: それを盗んで、荷星さんに なりすましたのです！
English: She stole it, and in doing so she became the Steel Samurai!

Japanese: 写真のトノサマンの中身は、 スタッフの女性だと？
English: So you're saying the Steel Samurai in the picture is really the assistant?

Japanese: そのとおりです！
English: That's correct, Your Honor!

Japanese: ホンキで言ってるとしたら、 大したドキョウだな。
English: If you're serious, you've got guts, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 写真を思い出してみたまえ。
English: Remember the photo?

Japanese: ‥‥トノサマンは、 足を引きずっているな？
English: Wasn't the Steel Samurai a little lame!? He was dragging his foot!

Japanese: う。 （‥‥イヤな予感‥‥）
English: Oh. (Uh oh. Bad feeling time.)

Japanese: 荷星のケガに関する証言を 思い出してみたまえ！
English: Remember the testimony concerning Powers's injury!!!

Japanese: 打ち合わせをしたメンバーは、 荷星とイブクロちゃん、
English: Powers and poor Hammer were at the run-through.

Japanese: それに、オバチャン。
English: Oh, and me.

Japanese: スタッフの女の子は？
English: What about the assistant?

Japanese: ああ。あの子はずっと大道具を 片づけていたからね。
English: Oh, her? She was cleaning up backdrops, I think.

Japanese: 荷星のケガのことは知らないョ。
English: She didn't know about Powers's ankle.

Japanese: スタッフは、荷星のケガを 知らなかった。
English: The assistant knew nothing of Mr. Power's injury!

Japanese: したがって、彼女が 荷星になりすましたとしたら‥‥
English: She could look the Steel Samurai look with that costume...

Japanese: この写真のように、 足を引きずっているはずがない！
English: But she couldn't walk the the Steel Samurai walk! She wouldn't have limped!

Japanese: ぐわあっ！
English: Ungh!

Japanese: カンゼンに イッポンとられたね‥‥。
English: That kind of takes the legs out from under that case...

Japanese: 成歩堂くん。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥では、約束どおり、 ペナルティを与えます。
English: I'm afraid I have to penalize you, as I warned.

Japanese: （うう、ヒドい目に あったぞ‥‥）
English: (Nobody knows... the trouble I've seen...)

Japanese: さて。‥‥もういいですか？ 成歩堂くん？
English: May we move on, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: （くそっ‥‥ここで あきらめるワケにはいかない！）
English: (Grr... this fight isn't over yet!)

Japanese: も、もう一度、 おねがいします‥‥。
English: If you would, Your Honor, one more chance, please.

Japanese: ‥‥わかりました。 では。
English: Very well. Go ahead.

Japanese: それは、小学生の男の子です！
English: It was the grade-school boy!

Japanese: な、なんで誰も 何も言ってくれないんですか？
English: Why isn't anyone saying anything?

Japanese: ‥‥ハッ！
English: Bah!

Japanese: では、キミは、 本気で主張する気かね。
English: Are you seriously suggesting the boy did it, Mr. Wright!?

Japanese: “小学生の子供が、ベテラン アクション俳優をヤリで刺殺！”
English: "Grade-school boy kills veteran action star with spear!"

Japanese: だいいち、凶器のヤリは、 トノサマンが持っていたのだ。
English: For one thing, it was the Steel Samurai holding the murder weapon.

Japanese: この少年に、いったい何が できたというのか！
English: Would you care to explain just how the boy was involved?

Japanese: （‥‥ま、たしかに、ちょっと ムリがあったかな‥‥）
English: (Hmm... maybe that was a dumb move on my part.)

Japanese: ちょっとどころじゃないよ、 なるほどくん！
English: "Maybe"? "Maybe"!? How about "absolutely," Nick!

Japanese: 警備をしていた オバチャンですよ、モチロン！
English: It was the security lady! Wendy Oldbag!

Japanese: な、なんだって！
English: W-who!?

Japanese: この写真のトノサマンは、 足を引きずっている。
English: The Steel Samurai is dragging his leg in this picture.

Japanese: つまり、午前中の打ち合わせで、 荷星さんがケガをしたことを、
English: That means whoever was in the suit knew about that morning's injury.

Japanese: ‥‥この人物は 知っていたことになる！
English: Maybe because... they had been watching the action scene run-through!

Japanese: 被害者と荷星さん以外に、 そのケガを知っていたのは、１人！
English: There was only one person other than Powers and Hammer who knew about the injury.

Japanese: オバチャンしかいません！
English: The security lady, Oldbag!
W-what!? Whippersnapper!

Japanese: 静粛に！　静粛に！
English: Order! Order!

Japanese: そ、そうなんですか オバチャン！
English: I-is this true, Oldbag!?

Japanese: き、気やすくオバチャンて 呼ぶんじゃないョ！
English: Oldbag!? That's Ms. Oldbag to you!

Japanese: オバチャンは、正門の前で、 １人で警備をしていた。
English: Ms. Oldbag was standing guard alone at the main gate.

Japanese: 逆に言えば、それは アリバイがないということです。
English: She was by herself... in other words, she has no alibi!

Japanese: 少しのあいだ、持ち場を離れ、 トノサマンの着ぐるみを盗んで‥‥
English: She could have briefly left her post to steal the the Steel Samurai costume...

Japanese: 殺人現場の第１スタジオに 行くことができたはずです！
English: Then slipped into Studio One, the scene of the murder!

Japanese: な、なぜ、わざわざ 着ぐるみを着たんですか？
English: W-why would she go through the trouble of wearing the Steel Samurai costume!?

Japanese: カンタンなことですよ。
English: Simple, Your Honor.

Japanese: ゲートのカメラに写真を 撮られないようにするためです。
English: She knew the camera at the gate would take her picture.

Japanese: 着ぐるみを着れば、荷星さんに ツミをかぶせることができる！
English: If she was in his costume, she could point the finger at Mr. Powers!

Japanese: な、なるほど！ ‥‥見事なスイリです‥‥。
English: I see!
Excellent deductive reasoning, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: （よしよし。 ダイセイコウだな‥‥）
English: (Ohhh yeah, right here! Sherlock Holmes II, baby!)

Japanese: （おかしいな）
English: (That's odd.)

Japanese: （いつもなら、このへんで 御剣のヤツ‥‥）
English: (Isn't this the part where Edgeworth pounces...?)

Japanese: （シャキーンと“異議”を 申し立てるはずなのに‥‥？）
English: (Doesn't he usually jump up with an objection and some new damning evidence...?)

Japanese: いかがですか？ ‥‥御剣検事‥‥？
English: Well, Mr. Edgeworth? Does the prosecution have an opinion on this matter?

Japanese: 今のところ、気のきいた 異議が思いつかない。
English: The prosecution has no meaningful objections at this time.

Japanese: ちょ、ちょ、ちょっと！ どういうことョ！
English: W-w-what!? What's that supposed to mean!?

Japanese: みんな、オバチャンがハンニン だって言うの！
English: Oh, so you all think I did it? Is that it!?

Japanese: ミッちゃん！ ダマってないで、助けなさいよ！
English: Edgey-boy! Don't just sit there, do something!

Japanese: （さて。どうする？ 追い打ちをかけておくか？）
English: (What's my move? Maybe now's my chance to take this the whole way?)

Japanese: （なりゆき上、ハンニン呼ばわり したけど‥‥）
English: (I guess this does kind of make it look like she's the killer.)

Japanese: （ちょっとカワイソウな 気もするな‥‥）
English: (Funny, I feel bad for her all of a sudden.)

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: たしかに、この証人にも、 犯行のチャンスはあったようです。
English: It's true that the witness could conceivably have committed this crime.

Japanese: しかし、相手はアクションスター です。
English: Yet we must not forget that the victim was an action hero.

Japanese: オバチャンでは、体力的に ムリがあるでしょうね‥‥。
English: As formidable as her personality may be, I doubt she'd be strong enough.

Japanese: （まずい！　このままじゃ、 荷星さんが犯人になってしまう）
English: (Uh oh! If he removes her from consideration, that means Mr. Powers is it!)

Japanese: （オバチャンには悪いけど、 ここは心をオニにして‥‥）
English: (Sorry, windbag... but I don't have a choice.)

Japanese: 荷星さんを犯人とする コンキョは‥‥
English: The very same reasoning that makes Mr. Powers a suspect in this case...

Japanese: そのまま、このオバチャンにも 当てはまります！
English: can be used to cast doubt on Ms. Oldbag's actions on that day!

Japanese: ど、どうしてオバチャンが イブクロちゃんを‥‥！
English: B-but why would I do something so horrible to poor Hammer!?

Japanese: 動機が不明という点では、 荷星さんも同じでしょう？
English: You forget that Mr. Powers lacks a clear motive, too.

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 そのとおりですね‥‥。
English: Hmm... Indeed.

Japanese: （これで、オバチャンも 容疑者の仲間入り、だな）
English: (That did it. Now windbag is one of the suspects.)

Japanese: （ちょっとキノドクだけど、 荷星さんのためだ‥‥）
English: (No hard feelings... I hope.)

Japanese: 待ちなよ！
English: Wait just a minute!

Japanese: あの、スタジオに行った もう１人はどうなるんだい！
English: What about the other person who went to the studio!?

Japanese: オバチャンが写真のデータを 消しちゃった子供さ！
English: The boy! The one whose photo I erased!

Japanese: でも、小学校２、３年生の 少年なんでしょう？
English: He's only a grade schooler though, as you said. 2nd or 3rd grade, was it?

Japanese: カンケイないね！
English: Th-that doesn't matter!

Japanese: オバチャン、そのころにはもう、 お父ちゃんには負けなかったよ！
English: When I was that age, I could pin my old man in 10 seconds, tops!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。 いかがですか？　弁護人。
English: Hmm... Your thoughts, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: その少年は犯人ではありません。
English: That boy is not the killer.

Japanese: 何、言ってんだョ！
English: What!? How can you be so sure!

Japanese: コドモだからって、サベツは よくないと思うねオバチャンは！
English: Oh, or is it be nice to the kids and mean to your elders day? Whippersnapper!

Japanese: ‥‥証拠もあるんですよ。
English: I have proof.

Japanese: な、なんだって！
English: P-proof!?

Japanese: ‥‥それでは、 見せていただきましょう。
English: Indeed? Then let's see this proof, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: 少年が犯人ではないという 証拠を。
English: You have proof that shows the boy could not have committed this murder?

Japanese: その証拠は、これです！
English: This is my proof!

Japanese: 今、その証拠品は カンケイないでしょう。
English: I can't see how that has anything to do with this matter, Mr. Wright?

Japanese: 弁護人。
English: Mr. Wright.

Japanese: それほど証拠品がたくさん あるわけではないのですから、
English: There aren't that many pieces of evidence in the Court Record.

Japanese: 間違えないでいただきたい！
English: Please try not to pick the wrong ones!

Japanese: す、すみません。
English: S-sorry, Your Honor.

Japanese: では、もう一度 聞きましょう。
English: I'll ask again, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: ‥‥いいですか。
English: Your Honor!

Japanese: 凶器は、トノサマン・スピアー です。
English: The murder weapon was the Samurai Spear.

Japanese: そして、その凶器は、こうして 写真に写っています。
English: That very spear is shown here in this photo.

Japanese: ‥‥少年に、スピアーを 入手することは不可能です！
English: How could the boy have taken the spear? It's impossible!

Japanese: なるほど‥‥。
English: I see!

Japanese: どうですか？　証人。
English: Well, would the witness care to comment on this?
Mrph.

Japanese: （さすがに、返すコトバも ないみたいだな‥‥）
English: (The windbag... speechless. This has got to be a first.)

Japanese: 殺人現場は、第１スタジオ。
English: The scene of the murder, as we all know, was Studio One.

Japanese: ここに入るには、カードキーが 必要です。
English: However, you need a cardkey to enter Studio One!

Japanese: 部外者である少年が、 カードを持っているはずがない！
English: How could a boy with no formal relation to the studios have a cardkey!?
Very well!

Japanese: 本日の審理は、ここで 中断したいと思います。
English: This court will suspend proceedings on the current trial for today.

Japanese: 検察側は、この証人‥‥ ええと、名前、なんでしたかな？
English: Mr. Edgeworth, please find out more about your witness, Ms. Windy... what was her name?

Japanese: “大場なにがし”だったと キオクしている。
English: Something "Oldbag," Your Honor.

Japanese: 検察側は、大場さんの取り調べを 行うように！
English: Then the prosecution will look further into this Oldbag before we continue!

Japanese: ‥‥では、 本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!

Japanese: ちょっと、待っとくれよ！
English: W-wait a second!

Japanese: そこまで言われちゃ、 ダマってらんないわネ！
English: I'm not going to just sit here while you run off barking up the wrong tree... me!

Japanese: オバチャン、 しゃべっちゃうよォォ‥‥
English: I'm talking!

Japanese: あのオバチャン、 まだしゃべる気なの‥‥？
English: Oh, great, stop the presses! The windbag wants to talk!

Japanese: 証人！ ‥‥どういうことですか？
English: Ms. Oldbag! What is this all about?

Japanese: 何か、証言していないことが あるのですか！
English: Have you omitted something from your testimony?

Japanese: 実はオバチャンね。 口止めされてたことがあんのョ！
English: Actually, if you must know, there's something I was told not to talk about.

Japanese: く、口止めですって！
English: N-not to talk about!?

Japanese: い、いったい、誰に！
English: By whom!?

Japanese: （あれ？　御剣じゃないのか‥‥）
English: (Huh? You mean it wasn't Edgeworth who told her not to talk...?)

Japanese: と、とにかく、《証言》を しなさい！
English: W-well, testify!

Japanese: ～口止めされていたこと～
English: -- My Lips Were Sealed --

Japanese: オバチャン、英都撮影所から 口止めされていたことがあるのョ。
English: Global Studios wanted me to keep quiet about something.

Japanese: 事件があった日、実はあの撮影所 には、他にも人がいたのよねェ。
English: There were... some other people at the studios on the day of the murder.

Japanese: その人たち、“ワレワレは事件に 関係ないから”だってサ。
English: They said they had "nothing to do with it," see?

Japanese: “撮影所にはいなかったことに してくれタマエ”なんて言って。
English: So they told me to just pretend they "hadn't been at the studios that day."

Japanese: オバチャンがうたがわれるんなら、 アイツらも巻き込んじゃおうかね！
English: But if you're going to go accusing me, I'm not letting them get away scot free!

Japanese: ‥‥しょ、証人！
English: M-Ms. Oldbag!

Japanese: なんでそんなダイジなこと、 今まで言わなかったんですか！
English: This is crucial information! Why did you keep this from the court until now!?

Japanese: だーかーらー。
English: Ain't you been listening?

Japanese: 口止めされてたんだから しょうがないでショ！
English: They told me to shut my trap, and I always do what I'm told.

Japanese: （トホホ‥‥と思っているに 違いないな‥‥）
English: (No, this isn't a bad dream, Your Honor. Witness the power of the Oldbag...)

Japanese: 成歩堂くん‥‥《尋問》。
English: Mr. Wright... Your cross-examination.

Japanese: ～口止めされていたこと～
English: -- My Lips Were Sealed --

Japanese: オバチャン、英都撮影所から 口止めされていたことがあるのョ。
English: Global Studios wanted me to keep quiet about something.

Japanese: 事件があった日、実はあの撮影所 には、他にも人がいたのよねェ。
English: There were... some other people at the studios on the day of the murder.

Japanese: その人たち、“ワレワレは事件に 関係ないから”だってサ。
English: They said they had "nothing to do with it," see?

Japanese: “撮影所にはいなかったことに してくれタマエ”なんて言って。
English: So they told me to just pretend they "hadn't been at the studios that day."

Japanese: オバチャンがうたがわれるんなら、 アイツらも巻き込んじゃおうかね！
English: But if you're going to go accusing me, I'm not letting them get away scot free!

Japanese: オバチャンを追いつめたの、 大正解だったね！
English: Nick! I guess pressing her was the right thing to do!

Japanese: あのオバチャン、かくしごとが 好きみたいだから、
English: For an old windbag, she sure likes keeping secrets...

Japanese: とにかく、情報を引き出そうよ。
English: Let's get as much information as we can!

Japanese: 口止め‥‥ですか？
English: So, you were told not to talk?

Japanese: そうだョ！　撮影所や、テレビ局の おエラいさん連中だョ！
English: That's right! By the studio and TV bigwigs, no less!

Japanese: き、聞いてないぞワレワレは！
English: Why didn't I hear about this!?

Japanese: そりゃまあ、 みんなで隠してたからね。
English: Don't be too hard on yourself, sonny. We were all in on it.

Japanese: それは、ダレですかッ！
English: W-who were these people!?

Japanese: まず、カントクとプロデューサー。
English: Well, the director and the producer, for starters...

Japanese: カントク‥‥？
English: The... director?

Japanese: オカシイと思うべきだった‥‥。
English: We should have known something was fishy!

Japanese: 監督なしで、アクションの 打ち合わせなど‥‥
English: How could they have done a run-through of their action scene without a director?

Japanese: するはずがないッ！
English: Of course!

Japanese: そりゃそうよねェ。
English: Yes, well, I was surprised no one asked about it.

Japanese: 彼らは、どこにいたんですか？
English: So, where were these people?

Japanese: カントクは、午前中は スタッフエリアで打ち合わせ。
English: The director was in the Employee Area all morning for the run-through.

Japanese: お昼からは、プロデューサーと 合流して、会議してたみたい。
English: He joined the producer around lunchtime and they had a meeting after that.

Japanese: どこで！
English: Where!

Japanese: 第２スタジオの、コテージでね。
English: Oh, in the Studio Two trailer.

Japanese: だ、第２スタジオ‥‥？ （初耳だぞ‥‥）
English: S-Studio Two...!? (There was a Studio Two...?)

Japanese: 上面図でいうと‥‥、
English: Well, if you look at the guidemap...

Japanese: ここだね。
English: Here it is.

Japanese: ゲートを通って、左に ずっと行ったところ。
English: You go through the gate and all the way to the left.

Japanese: （‥‥あの、サルのクビが 落ちていた方面か‥‥）
English: (The path where the monkey's head was fallen over.)

Japanese: どうですか、成歩堂くん。 ‥‥まだ尋問しますか？
English: Well, Mr. Wright... Would you like to continue the cross-examination...?

Japanese: （聞くべきコトは聞いたと思うけど どうしよう‥‥）
English: (I think I've already asked all my questions, but...)

Japanese: オバチャン！ ‥‥殺人事件なんですよ！
English: Ms. Oldbag! This is a trial for murder!

Japanese: わかってるわョ。
English: I know that, silly!

Japanese: でも、話を聞くと、たしかに 事件とは関係なさそうだったし。
English: Still, they were pretty convincing about having nothing to do with it.

Japanese: ちょっとおこづかいも もらっちゃったんでさ‥‥。
English: And... they gave me a little bonus on the side.

Japanese: （おこづかいもらって、結局 バラしちゃうんだからなぁ‥‥）
English: (So, you got your "bonus" and you still talked...)

Japanese: ジッサイには、 撮影所にいたわけですね！
English: So they were actually in the studio!?

Japanese: ああ。１日中ね。
English: Yes, the whole day.

Japanese: 事件を知って、警察が来る前に あわてて帰っていったョ。
English: When they heard about the murder, they beat it before the cops came.

Japanese: ぶつぶつ‥‥（あとで しめ上げてやるぞ‥‥）
English: *grumble* (I'll kill this woman, I swear it...)

Japanese: （御剣検事もごリップクか）
English: (I feel sorry for Edgeworth, almost. She really left him hanging on this one.)

Japanese: “アイツら”‥‥？
English: "Them"...?

Japanese: （‥‥聞くべきコトは聞いたと 思うけど、どうしよう‥‥）
English: (Hmm. I think I've asked all the questions I have to ask...)

Japanese: （そうだな‥‥。集められるだけ、 情報を聞き出しておくか！）
English: (I guess there's no harm in getting as much info as I can!)

Japanese: 裁判長。
English: Your Honor.

Japanese: 英都撮影所には、事件当日、 他にも人がいました。
English: We have learned there were others at Global Studios on the day in question.

Japanese: カントクに、プロデューサー。 そして、えーと、おエラいさん。
English: The director and the producer and, er, some bigwigs, were all present.

Japanese: 彼らは、取り調べも 受けていない！
English: Yet, as we stand here, they have not been questioned!

Japanese: この時点で、被告人・荷星さんに 判決を下すことは不可能です！
English: I hold that it is impossible to declare a verdict on the defendant, Mr. Powers!

Japanese: ふむう‥‥。
English: Hmm...

Japanese: 弁護人の主張を認めます！
English: The court acknowledges the defense's point.

Japanese: 検察側は、まずこの証人、 大場さん。
English: The prosecution will gather more information about the witness, Ms. Oldbag...

Japanese: そして、証言に出てきた 人たちを取り調べるように！
English: and more information about these other people we have just been told of!

Japanese: 了解した。

English: I understand, Your Honor.

Japanese: 本日の荷星　三郎の審理は、 ここまでとします。
English: This ends the day's proceedings in the trial of Mr. Will Powers.

Japanese: ‥‥では、 本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!

Japanese: １０月１８日　午後１時４分 地方裁判所　被告人第３控え室
English: October 18, 1:04 PM District Court Defendant Lobby No. 3

Japanese: 本当に、 ありがとうございました。
English: Th-thank you so much, Mr. Wright.

Japanese: あなた方に依頼したのは 大正解でした。
English: I was right to ask you to defend me.

Japanese: いやいや‥‥、恐縮です。 なーんて。
English: Aww, really, it's nothing. *grin*
Oh, or should I... *sniff*

Japanese: ぼくたちは、これから また撮影所の方へ調査に行きます。
English: We'll be going down to the studios to do some more investigation.

Japanese: 例の、カントクやらプロデューサー やらを、調べなくちゃ。
English: We have to find out more about this director and producer.

Japanese: きっと彼らは、次の法廷で 証人として出てきます。
English: They'll be turning up in the next trial as witnesses for certain.

Japanese: 尋問の材料を集めないと、ね。
English: So now's my chance to get material for the cross-examination!

Japanese: でもさあ、なるほどくん。
English: So, Nick...

Japanese: 結局、トノサマンの中に入っていた のは、誰だったんだろ？
English: Have we figured out just who it was in that Steel Samurai costume?

Japanese: あのオバチャンだったのかな？
English: Could it really have been old windbag?

Japanese: どう思います？　荷星さん。
English: What do you think, Mr. Powers?

Japanese: 違うと思いますよ。
English: I don't think it was her, really.

Japanese: あたしも違うと思うな。
English: Neither do I, Nick.

Japanese: だ、だから、あれは時間を かせぐために、
English: Y-yeah, I know! Look, I was just buying time back there.

Japanese: オバチャンに悪役になって もらっただけだからね‥‥。
English: Someone had to be the bad guy for a bit to take the pressure off Mr. Powers.

Japanese: ちょっと、カワイソウ‥‥。
English: Poor old windbag... I feel kinda sorry for her.

Japanese: ま。いいだろ。
English: Well, she wasn't winning any points in there, with or without my accusation.

Japanese: じゃ、ぼくたちはすぐ、 英都撮影所に行きますから。
English: Okay, let's get down to the studios.

Japanese: またあとで、おじゃましますね！ ‥‥留置所に。
English: Right! We'll be back to visit you soon...

Japanese: は、はッ。 ‥‥恐縮です。
English: Th-thanks. Thanks, guys. *sniff*

Japanese: つづく
English: To be continued.

Japanese: そこまで！
English: That's enough!

Japanese: 本法廷は、これ以上の審議の 必要性を認めません。
English: This court sees no reason to further prolong the trial.

Japanese: さらに、被告人に対する判決を 決める時間も、必要としません。
English: Nor is there any need for more time to decide the case against the defendant.

Japanese: これは、きわめて明白な事件です。 疑問の余地はない！
English: This case is extremely clear. I see no room for mis- interpretation of the facts.

Japanese: （そ、そんなバカな‥‥）
English: (W-what!? I see plenty of room! It's still wide open... no?)

Japanese: 被告人、荷星　三郎に 判決を言い渡します！
English: This court finds the defendant, Mr. Will Powers...

Japanese: 被告人はこれより身柄を 司直に預けるものとし、
English: The accused will surrender to the court immediately,

Japanese: １ヶ月以内に、高等裁判所において 通常裁判を受けることとします。
English: to be held pending trial at a higher court within a month from today's date.

Japanese: では、本日はこれにて閉廷！
English: That is all. The court is adjourned!
